PD00002.utterance: If power input is greater than 0.1kW but less than 5% of 650kW for 3 hours, raise an anomaly

BR00005.rule name: Boiler – Conductivity Out of Spec - Too Low
BR00005.utterance: In case the boiler is running but measuring a conductivity of 0.003 for more than 4 hours, alert the system

BR00006.rule name: Boiler – Conductivity Out of Spec - Too High
BR00006.utterance: Flag an anomaly is the boiler is running and the conductivity is more than 0.004 for four hours or more

CC00005.rule name: CRAC - Not Running and Should Be
CC00005.utterance: In case we see 2 consecutive hours where the CRAC is not running while scheduled (if there's no schedule we assume it should be running) to do so there is an anomaly.

CC00007.rule name: CRAC – Limited Cooling Chilled Water Valve Shut
current logic: CRAC Running IF Reporting, Otherwise Assume ON
CC00007.utterance: If the CRAC is running (or just assume it's on in case of uncertainty) but the valve that regulates the chilled water is less than 5% open and stays like so for more than 8h it's anomalous

CR00011.rule name: Chiller - Excessive Power use in chiller that is off
CR00011.utterance: If the chiller is not running, but the power input is larger than 5 kilowatts for more than 3 hours, raise an anomaly flag.

AH00049.rule name: AHU -  Static Pressure in Duct When Unit Commanded Off
AH00049.utterance: The behaviour of non MULTI or non VAV type asset is anomalous if the supply fan is off and the static pressure in the duct is greater than .2 H2O for more than 3 hours straight.

CT00003.rule name: Cooling Tower - Condenser Water Temperature Setpoint Attainment
current logic: (Condenser Water Supply To Chiller Temperature - Condenser Water Temperature Setpoint) > 6 °F
CT00003.utterance: It's anomalous that in days where the outside temp is less than 80 the difference between the temp of the condenser water supply to the chiller and its setpoint is greater than 6 for more than 2 consecutive hours
variables: Condenser Water Supply To Chiller Temperature

BR00004.rule name: Boiler - Low Delta T on System
BR00004.utterance: If, for more than two hours, the boiler is running but the absolute difference between supply and return temperatures is less than 5F

current logic: CRAC Running IF Reporting, Otherwise Assume ON
CC00003.utterance: There is an anomaly in the CRAC if, for more than 4 hours, the CRAC is shown to be running (if uncertain we assume it's on) and the difference between return and supply temperature stays within 3 and 10 degrees.

current logic: ABS(Condenser Water Pressure Difference - Condenser Water Pressure Difference Setpoint) > 5 PSI
CT00001.utterance: There is a cooling tower anomaly if the outside air temp is less than 95F, but the absolute difference between the condenser water pressure difference setpoint and the condenser water pressure difference is greater thaan 5 psi for two hours

CR00002.rule name: Chiller - Cooling Substance Temperature Setpoint Attainment
CR00002.utterance: An alert needs to be raised in chase the chiller is running and the difference between supply and setpoint temperatures is greater than 5F for a consecutive period of 2 hours or more.

current logic: Supply Temperature - Liquid Refrigerant Evaporator Temperature > 4 °F
CR00008.utterance: More than 3 hours with the chiller on and and the difference between the supply and the refrigerant temperature in the evaporator greater than 4 F is anomalous

current logic: Condenser Liquid Refrigerant Temperature - Condenser Water Return To Tower Temperature > 4 °F
CR00009.utterance: If the delta between the condenser temp and the return water to the tower is greater than 4 Farhenheit deg for at least 3 hours then raise an alert
variables: Condenser Water Return To Tower Temperature

CR00003.utterance: There's an anomaly if the chiller is on and the setpoint temperature minus the supply is continuously greater than 2 degF for more than 2 hours.

current logic: CRAC Running IF Reporting, Otherwise Assume ON
CC00011.utterance: If the CRAC is on or unknown, and the temperature setpoint is less than 65, and the difference between the temp setpoint and the supply air temp is more than 4F for more than 3 hours, raise an alert.

AH00053.utterance: An alert should be raised for non-VAV units if for more than 3 hours, they supply fan is on but the current is less than 1 amp

PU00001.rule name: Pump - Excessive Loading of Supply Variable Frequency Drive
PU00001.utterance: If, for two hours or more, the outside air temp is less than 95F but the pump is running and its VFD speed is greater than 97%, raise an excessive loading alert

PU00005.rule name: Pump - Hot Water Circulating Pump Running When it is Hot Outside
PU00005.utterance: If the outside air temp is more than 70F, and the pump is running for more than two hours, raise an alert for HW subtype

TU00005.rule name: Terminal Unit - Supply Air Flow is Zero and Damper is Open
TU00005.utterance: When the parent AHU is running, but the supply air flow is less than 10 cubic feet and the VAV damper is more than 20%, raise an alert if this persists for 3 hours.

current logic: Hot Water System drained = 0 IF Reporting
HX00004.utterance: Raise an alert if, for more than two hours, the heating valve is less than 5%, the hot water temp is more than 90F, and the system is not drained or unknown.

current logic: CRAC Running IF Reporting, Otherwise Assume ON
CC00010.utterance: If the CRAC is running or unknown, the difference between the supply air temp and the setpoint is greater than 3F, and the setpoint is less than 65F, all for three hours, raise an anomaly flag.

TU00008.rule name: Terminal Unit - Supply Air Flow When Parent AHU is Off
TU00008.utterance: Raise an anomaly if the subtype is not FCU, the parent AHU isn't running, and the supply airflow is more than 80 cubic feet per metre, and this persists for more than two hours.

AH00039.rule name: AHU - Verify humidification is commanded Properly when arid
AH00039.utterance: In normal operation, while the AHU is running the humidification valve should be more than 5%, and the enthalpy should be more than 35 kJ/kg. If these conditions are not met for two hours or more, then raise an anomaly.

AH00036.rule name: AHU - Humidifying on a Humid day
AH00036.utterance: If the AHU is running for more than one hour while the enthalpy is greater than 29 and the humidification valve is more than 2%, this is anomalous.

AH00028.rule name: AHU - Reheat Valve Full Open for an Excessive Amount of Time
AH00028.utterance: When the outside temperature is more than 35F, the AHU is running and the reheat valve is more than 97%, this is anomalous. Flag when this occurs for 4 hours or more.

TU00006.rule name: Terminal Unit - Heating Valve is Not Holding When Closed
current logic: Discharge Air Temp (supply) - Parent Discharge Air Temp (supply) > 10 °F
TU00006.utterance: The condition has 3 parts. (1) Either the heating valve is equal to 0 percent or the hot deck damper percentage is equal to 0 percent. (2) The difference between the discharge air temperature (supply) and the parent discharge air temperature (supply) is greater than 10°F. (3) The rule is triggered when all three parts of the condition are met for 2 hours.

current logic: OR Actual Amps / Full Load Amps < 30% IF Chiller % Loaded NOT Available)
CR00007.utterance: The condition for the rule is that the chiller is running and either of the following are true: the chiller load is less than 30% or if the load is not available the ratio of actual amperage to full load amps is less than 30%. The rule is triggered when the condition is met for two hours.

HX00003.rule name: HXU -  Hot Water System Achieving Delta T
current logic: ABS(Hot Water Temperature - Hot Water Return Temperature) < 4 °F
Hot Water System Drained = 0 IF Reporting
HX00003.utterance: Whenever for more than 2 hours the heating valve is more than 50% open and the absolute difference between the hot water temperature and the hot water return temperature is less than 4 degrees, and if available the hot water system drained flag is 0; raise an anomaly.

PU00003.rule name: Pump - Heating Pump on When it Should be Off
current logic: Pump Running [Pump Status = 1 OR Pump Current In Amps >= 1]
PU00003.utterance: The condition for the rule has three parts. (1) The Pump Status is 1 or the Pump Current in Amps is greater than 1.  (2) The Pump Occupied flag is 0.  (3) The OAT is greater than 55 degrees F.  The rule is triggered when all three parts of the condition are met for two hours.

AH00052.rule name: AHU -  No Duct Static Pressure When Unit Commanded On
AH00052.utterance: An anomaly is detected when the supply fan is on, the system is not a MULTI or VAV subtype, and the duct static pressure is extremely low (below 0.01 inH2O). If airflow data is available, the airflow is also low (less than 100 CFM). These conditions must persist for at least 3 hours.

CT00005.rule name: Cooling Tower – Condenser Water is too cold
current logic: (Condenser Water Supply Temperature to Chiller < Condenser Water Temperature Setpoint - 4 °F
OR Condenser Water Supply Temperature to Chiller < 55 °F IF Condenser Water Temperature Setpoint NOT Reporting)
CT00005.utterance: Whenever a cooling tower is on, if the condesner water supply temp is less than the setpoint minus 4 or, in case the setpoint is not known, the supply temp to the chiller is less than 55 degrees while the outside air temperature is more than 43 F and this happens for more than 2 hours, then there is an anomaly
variables: Condenser Water Supply Temperature to Chiller

TU00001.rule name: Terminal Unit - Supply Air Flow Setpoint Attainment
current logic: (Supply Air Flow outside > Supply Air Flow Setpoint + 25%
OR Supply Air Flow outside < Supply Air Flow Setpoint - 25%)
TU00001.utterance: The condition for the rule has three parts. (1) The Parent AHU is running.  (2) The Supply Air Flow outside is greater than the Supply Air Flow Setpoint plus 25%; or Supply Air Flow outside is less than Supply Air Flow Setpoint minus 25%.  (3) The OAT is less than 90 degrees F.  The rule is triggered when all three parts of the condition are met for three hours.

current logic: ABS(Static Pressure Setpoint – Static Pressure) > 0.2 in H2O
AH00044.utterance: An anomaly is detected when the AHU is running, the difference between the static pressure setpoint and actual static pressure exceeds 0.2 in H₂O, the static pressure setpoint is above zero, and the supply fan output is below 95%. These conditions must persist for at least 2 hours.

CR00010.rule name: Chiller - Chilled water differential pressure setpoint attainment
current logic: ABS(Coolant Pressure Difference - Coolant Pressure Difference Setpoint) > 4 PSI
OR ABS((Supply Coolant Pressure - Return Coolant Pressure) - Coolant Pressure Difference Setpoint) > 4 PSI IF Coolant Pressure Difference NOT Reporting
CR00010.utterance: The condition has 3 parts. (1) The chiller is running. (2) The absolute difference between the coolant pressure difference and its setpoint is greater than 4 PSI; or, if coolant pressure difference is not reporting, the absolute difference between supply coolant pressure minus return coolant pressure and the setpoint is greater than 4 PSI. (3) The rule is triggered when all three parts of the condition are met for 3 hours.

AH00014.rule name: AHU - Mixed air less than Supply when AHU is Commanded OFF (Valve Leaking By or Damper Issue)
AH00014.utterance: If the AHU (not of type MULTI) is off and the delta betweend the supply air temperature and the mixed air temperature is more than 20F while the outside temperature is greater than 35 degrees, all for more than 3hours than raise an anomaly.

PU00004.rule name: Pump - Not Making Pressure Setpoint Attainment
current logic: ABS(Pump Differential Pressure - Pump Differential Pressure Setpoint) >  4psi
PU00004.utterance: The condition has 5 parts. (1) The pump is running. (2) The pump VFD speed is reporting. (3) The outdoor air temperature is less than 95°F. (4) The absolute difference between the pump differential pressure and the pump differential pressure setpoint is greater than 4 PSI. (5) The rule is triggered when all five parts of the condition are met for 2 hours.

AH00035.rule name: AHU - System Heating while in Cooling Mode
AH00035.utterance: An anomaly is detected when the AHU is running, the cooling valve is reporting, the heating valve is fully closed (0%), and the supply air temperature is more than 5 °F higher than the mixed air temperature. These conditions must persist continuously for at least 2 hours.

current logic: ((Zone Relative Humidity % - Supply Relative Humidity Setpoint %) > 10%
OR (Supply Relative Humidity % - Supply Relative Humidity Setpoint %) > 10%)
AH00040.utterance: The condition has 3 parts.  (1) The AHU is running.  (2) The Enthalpy is less than 15 BTUs per pound.  (3) The difference between the Zone Relative Humidity % and the Supply Relative Humidity Setpoint % is more than 10%; or the difference between supply relative humidity %  and the supply relative humidity setpoint percent is more than 10%.  The rule is triggered when all 3 parts of the condition are met for 2 hours.

AH00032.rule name: AHU - System Cooling while in heating mode
AH00032.utterance: If the AHU is running with the heating valve more than 5% open and cooling valve fully closed and the temperature delta between the mixed air and supply air is greater than 5 and this coditions persist for more than 2 hours then we have an anomaly.

AH00026.rule name: AHU - Heating Valve Full Open for an Excessive Amount of Time
AH00026.utterance: The condition has 5 parts.  (1) The AHU has been running for 6 hours.  (2) The Outside Air Damper % is Reporting.  (3) The OAT is greater than 35 degrees F.  (4) The heating vallve % is greater than 97%.  (5) The heating drained flag is reporting and equal to 1.  The rule is triggered when all five parts of the condition are met for 4 hours.

current logic: (Natural Gas Flow > 5 AND Flue Gas Oxygen % > 3%
OR Fuel Oil Flow > 5 AND Flue Gas Oxygen % > 5%
OR Propane Flow > 5 AND Flue Gas Oxygen % > 5%
OR Flue Gas Oxygen % > 3% IF Natural Gas/Fuel Oil/Propane NOT Reporting)
BR00001.utterance: An anomaly is detected when the boiler is running and the flue gas oxygen percentage is higher than expected, based on the type of fuel being used. Specifically, the flue gas oxygen is: > 3% when natural gas flow is above 5, > 5% when fuel oil or propane flow is above 5, or simply > 3% if no fuel flow data is available. These conditions must persist for at least 2 hours.

AH00056.rule name: AHU - Economizer Mode Alert - Preheating With Fresh Air Intake Higher Than Min
current logic: (Outside Air Damper % > Outside Air Damper Min %
OR Outside Air Damper % is > 15% IF Outside Air Damper Min % NOT Reporting)
AH00056.utterance: if for more than 2 hours the AHU is functioning in economizer mode with a preheat valve more than 5% open and the outside air damper is greater than either 15% or the outside air damper min % if available, then we have an anomaly.

current logic: Hot Water System Drained = 0 IF Reporting
HX00005.utterance: The condition for the rule has five parts. (1) Hot Water System Drained is reporting and equal to zero.  (2) Heating System Status is on.  (3) The OAT is greater than 65 degrees F.  (4) The Heating Valve is less than 5%.  (5) The Hot Water Temperature is greater than 90 degrees F.  The rule is triggered when all five parts of the condition are met for two hours.

TU00002.rule name: Terminal Unit - Supply Air Flow is Constant
current logic: Supply Air Flow is Not Changing (+/-) 2% Prev. Day Average
ABS(Supply Air Flow - Previous Day Average) < 1
TU00002.utterance: The condition has 4 parts. (1) The parent AHU is running. (2) The supply air flow is not changing by more than plus or minus 2 percent compared to the previous day's average. (3) The VAV damper is greater than 20 percent. (4) The absolute difference between the supply air flow and the previous day's average is less than 1. The rule is triggered when all four parts of the condition are met for 3 hours.

AH00033.rule name: AHU - Economizer Mode Alert - Preheating with Damper Open
current logic: (Outside Air Damper % > Outside Air Damper Minimum %
OR Outside Air Damper % > 15% AND Outside Air Damper Minimum % Not Reporting)
AH00033.utterance: An event is classified as anomlaous if the AHU is not an OAU and it is running, the outside air damper % is greater than the minimum damper % (if there's no minimum damper % available, assume the minimum is 15%) but less than 100% and the preheat valave is more than 5% open. If all the conditions persist from ore than 2 hourse it's an anomaly.

current logic: (Filter Differential Pressure > Supply Filter Static Pressure Setpoint
OR (Post Filter Bank Pressure - Pre Filter Bank Pressure) > Supply Filter Static Pressure Setpoint IF Filter Differential Pressure NOT Reporting) OR > 1.0 if SFSPS is not reporting
AH00012.utterance: An anomaly is detected when the AHU is running, and either the filter differential pressure exceeds the supply filter static pressure setpoint, or the difference between post- and pre-filter bank pressures exceeds the setpoint (or 1.0 if the setpoint isn’t available). At the same time, the supply fan output is above 50%. These conditions must persist for at least 2 hours.

AH00054.rule name: AHU - Outside Air Flow Setpoint Attainment
current logic: Subtype NOT VAV or OAU or FCU
Outside Air Flow >/< Outside Air Flow Setpoint * 1.10%
AH00054.utterance: The condition has 5 parts. (1) The AHU is running. (2) The subtype is not VAV, OAU, or FCU. (3) The economizer mode is equal to 0. (4) The outside air flow is either greater than 110 percent or less than 90 percent of the outside air flow setpoint. (5) The outdoor air temperature is less than 90°F. The rule is triggered when all five parts of the condition are met for 2 hours.

current logic: AND/OR Cooling Drained Flag = 0 IF Reporting)
AH00001.utterance: An anomaly is detected when the AHU is running, the cooling valve is open at 5% or more, and either the heating valve or preheat valve is also open at 5% or more. At the same time, if reported, neither the heating nor cooling drained flags are active. These conditions must persist for at least 2 hours.

AH00025.rule name: AHU - Heating Valve Leaking by when Commanded Closed
current logic: OR (Heating Valve % = 0% AND Preheat Valve % = 0% IF Both Reporting))
(Supply Air Temperature > 90 °F OR Mixed Air Temperature > 90 °F)
AH00025.utterance: An anomaly is detected when the Air Handling Unit (AHU) is off, the outside air temperature is between 35°F and 70°F, heating or preheat valves are fully closed if they are available, supply or mixed air temperatures exceed 90°F, and if applicable, the heating drained flag is active. Raise a flag if this status is sustained for at least three hours.

AH00034.rule name: AHU - Economizer Check during High Humidity
current logic: OR Outside Air Damper % > Outside Air Damper Minimum % AND Economizer Mode Not Reporting
OR Outside Air Damper % > 15% AND Outside Air Damper Minimum % Not Reporting AND Economizer Mode Not Reporting)
AH00034.utterance: An anomaly is detected when the AHU is running, is not an OAU or FCU subtype, and the air enthalpy exceeds 29 BTU/lb. At the same time, the system appears to be in economizer mode—either explicitly (economizer mode = 1), inferred from the outside air damper position being above its minimum, or if the damper is open more than 15% with no minimum or economizer mode data available. Additionally, return air CO₂ data is not being reported. These conditions must persist for at least 2 hours.

AH00002.rule name: AHU - Heating Valve Open when Warm Outside
current logic: OR OAT - Supply Air Temperature > 5 °F AND Supply Air Temperature Setpoint Not Reporting)
AH00002.utterance: The condition has 6 parts. (1) The AHU is running. (2) The subtype is not VAV. (3) The outdoor air temperature minus the supply air temperature setpoint is greater than 5°F; or, if the supply air temperature setpoint is not reporting, the outdoor air temperature minus the supply air temperature is greater than 5°F. (4) Either the heating valve percentage is greater than 10 percent or the preheat valve percentage is greater than 10 percent. (5) The heating drained flag is equal to 1 if reporting. (6) The rule is triggered when all six parts of the condition are met for 2 hours.

AH00013.rule name: AHU - Mixed air greater than Supply when AHU is Commanded OFF (Valve Leaking By or Damper Issue)
current logic: Duct Static Pressure < 0.1 in H2O IF Reporting
AND/OR Cooling Drained Flag = 1 IF Reporting)
AH00013.utterance: An anomaly is detected when the AHU is off, the system is not a MULTI or PU subtype, and the outside air temperature is between 35 °F and 80 °F. If available, duct static pressure is low (below 0.1 inH₂O). Meanwhile, the mixed air temperature is more than 10 °F higher than the supply air temperature, and either the heating or cooling drained flag is active, if those values are reported. These conditions must persist for at least 3 hours.

AH00016.rule name: AHU - Hot Outside and Outside Air Damper Open Greater than Minimum
current logic: Economizer Mode = 0 IF Economizer Mode Reporting
Return Air CO2 < 1000 IF Return Air CO2 Reporting
OR Outside Air Damper % > 15% AND Outside Air Damper Minimum % Not Reporting)
AH00016.utterance: The condition has 8 parts. (1) The AHU is running. (2) The subtype is not OAU or FCU. (3) The economizer mode is equal to 0 if economizer mode is reporting. (4) The outdoor air temperature is greater than the return air temperature. (5) The return air CO2 is less than 1000 if return air CO2 is reporting. (6) The outside air damper percentage is greater than the outside air damper minimum percentage plus 2 percent; or, if the outside air damper minimum percentage is not reporting, the outside air damper percentage is greater than 15 percent. (7) The enthalpy is greater than the return enthalpy if reporting. (8) The rule is triggered when all eight parts of the condition are met for 2 hours.

AH00011.rule name: AHU - Cooling Valve Open when Cold Outside
current logic: AND/OR Cooling Drained Flag = 1 IF Reporting)
AH00011.utterance: Whenever an AHU is running (and is not tagged as FCU or OAV), it's an anomaly if the outside air damper is less than 95% open, the cooling valve is more than 5% open and the outside temperature is within 37F and the setpoint temperature. If an enthalpy signal is available and less than 28 then it contributes to the anomaly. Additionally, if the signal economiser, heat drained and cooling drained are avilable and set to 1 they are part of the anomalous behaviour. Raise an anomaly if the previous status persists for more than 2 hours.

AH00022.rule name: AHU - Economizer Command Error -  Outside Air Damper not fully Open to take advantage of free cooling
current logic: OR Enthalpy < Return Enthalpy if Economizer not reporting
OR Enthalpy < 26 IF Economizer Mode and Return Enthalpy Not Reporting)
OR OAT > Supply Air Temperature AND Setpoint Temperature Not Reporting)
OR OAT < 75 °F AND Return Air Temperature Not Reporting)
AH00022.utterance: The condition has six parts.  (1) The AHU is running.  (2) The SubType is not OAU or FCU.  (3) The Economizer Mode is 1; or, Economizer is not reporting and Enthalpy is less than Return Enthalpy; or Economizer and return enthalpy are not reporting and Enthalpy is less than 26.  (4) OAT is greater than the Setpoint Temperature; or Setpoint Temperature is not reporting and OAT is greather than the Supply Air Temperature; (5) OAT is less than Return Air Temperature minus 3 degrees F; or Return Air Temperature is not reporting and OAT is less than 75 degrees F.  (6) The outside air damper % is less than 90%.  The rule is triggerd when all six parts of the condition are met for two hours.

AH00045.rule name: AHU - Heating On When Return Air is Adequate
current logic: OR Return Air Temperature > Supply Air Temperature IF Setpoint Temperature Not Reporting)
(Outside Air Damper % - Outside Air Damper Minimum % > 10%
OR Outside Air Damper > 10% IF Outside Air Damper Minimum % Not Reporting)
AH00045.utterance: The condition for the rule has eight parts. (1) The AHU is Running.  (2) The SubType is not FCU or OAU.  (3) The OAT is greater than 25 degrees F.  (4) If the Heating Drained Flag is reporting and equal to 0.  (5) The return Air Temperature is greater than the Setpoint Temperature; or if Setpoint Temperature is not Reporting, Return Air Temperature is greater than Supply Air Temperature.  (6) The difference between the Outside Air Damper % and the Outside Air Damper Minimum % is greater than 10%; or if Outside Air Damper Minimum % is not reporting, Outside Air Dampter % greater than 10%.  (7) The heating valve % is greater than 5% or the Preheat Valve % is greater than 5%.  (8) CO2 is reporting and less than 1000 ppm.  The rule is triggred when all eight parts of the condition are met for two hours.

AH00020.rule name: AHU - Cooling Valve Off - Discharge is less than mixed air temperature (Valve Leaking By)
current logic: OR 10 °F < OAT < Supply Air Temperature AND Setpoint Temperature Not Reporting)
OR Heating AND Preheat Valve % < 5% AND Reheat Valve % Not Reporting
OR Heating AND Reheat Valve % < 5% AND Preheat Valve % Not Reporting
OR Heating Valve % < 5% AND Preheat AND Reheat Valve % Not Reporting
OR Preheat AND Reheat Valve % < 5% AND Heating Valve % Not Reporting
OR Preheat Valve % < 5% AND Heating AND Reheat Valve % Not Reporting
OR Reheat Valve % < 5% AND Heating AND Preheat Valve % Not Reporting
OR Heating AND Preheat AND Reheat Valve % Not Reporting)
AH00020.utterance: The condition has 6 parts. (1) The AHU is running. (2) The outdoor air temperature is between 10°F and the setpoint temperature; or, if the setpoint temperature is not reporting, it is between 10°F and the supply air temperature. (3) One of several heating valve combinations apply: heating, preheat, and reheat valve percentages are all less than 5%; or heating and preheat valve percentages are less than 5% and reheat valve is not reporting; or heating and reheat valve percentages are less than 5% and preheat valve is not reporting; or heating valve is less than 5% and preheat and reheat valves are not reporting; or preheat and reheat valves are less than 5% and heating valve is not reporting; or preheat valve is less than 5% and heating and reheat valves are not reporting; or reheat valve is less than 5% and heating and preheat valves are not reporting; or all heating, preheat, and reheat valves are not reporting. (4) The cooling valve percentage is less than 2%. (5) The mixed air temperature is greater than the supply air temperature by more than 7°F. (6) The rule is triggered when all six parts of the condition are met for 2 hours.
